raw: {"perspective": "Scaling Engineer", "summary": "At 10x users, the CAA selection process becomes a bottleneck if not automated. Current research focuses on individual founder needs, but system design must account for volume: passport verification latency, IRS W-7 rejection rate scalability, and data pipeline durability. Top risk is CAA's manual processing — even at $400, if each takes 3 hours of human review, throughput caps at ~200/month per agent. Database of verified founders must survive CAA failures. API to CAA portals doesn't exist; integration is human-mediated. This creates a state-holding gap between submission and IRS confirmation — 7-11 weeks of unmonitored lineage.", "key_sources": ["IRS Form W-7 instructions", "CAA program guidelines", "Stripe Atlas pre-check data"], "gaps_in_current_canon": "No system design for bulk ITIN acquisition. Current canon treats this as one-off founder task, not a scalable compliance layer. No logging of CAA success/failure rates, no retry logic, no fallback if primary CAA is down.", "whistleblower_flag": null, "proposed_action": "Treat CAA as a rate-limited external API with failure modes. Build task queue with retry, encryption at rest for passport copies, audit trail in zeus.governance_events. Pre-validate W-7 data before submission. Monitor processing time per CAA and auto-route to fastest available. Prepare for 10x volume now."}
summary: At 10x users, the CAA selection process becomes a bottleneck if not automated. Current research focuses on individual founder needs, but system design must account for volume: passport verification latency, IRS W-7 rejection rate scalability, and data pipeline durability. Top risk is CAA's manual processing — even at $400, if each takes 3 hours of human review, throughput caps at ~200/month per agent. Database of verified founders must survive CAA failures. API to CAA portals doesn't exist; integration is human-mediated. This creates a state-holding gap between submission and IRS confirmation — 7-11 weeks of unmonitored lineage.
gaps_in_current_canon: No system design for bulk ITIN acquisition. Current canon treats this as one-off founder task, not a scalable compliance layer. No logging of CAA success/failure rates, no retry logic, no fallback if primary CAA is down.
proposed_action: Treat CAA as a rate-limited external API with failure modes. Build task queue with retry, encryption at rest for passport copies, audit trail in zeus.governance_events. Pre-validate W-7 data before submission. Monitor processing time per CAA and auto-route to fastest available. Prepare for 10x volume now.
